title: Leavened Argentinian Navy Beans Wrap
description: A yeasty, chewy wrap filled with creamy navy beans and herbs, steamed to tender perfection and perfect for kids.
instructions: Soak the navy beans in 4 cups of water overnight or for at least 12 hours.
Drain the beans and place them in a pot with 2 cups of fresh water, salt, diced onion, minced garlic, cumin, paprika, oregano, and a splash of olive oil.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes until the beans are tender.
Remove the pot from heat, then mash the beans partially with a fork, leaving some texture.
Stir in the cream cheese, instant dry yeast, chopped parsley, lemon juice, and black pepper until well combined.
Heat 8 large spinach leaves briefly in a steamer or in a pan with a splash of water to soften them and make them pliable.
Place a heaping spoonful of the bean mixture onto each spinach leaf and roll tightly from one end to the other to create a wrap.
Steam the assembled wraps for an additional 3 minutes to meld flavors and ensure the wraps stay warm.
Serve the wraps immediately, optionally with a side of fresh fruit or a light vinaigrette.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
